Patch Set 2:

to me it looks like we just need a dedicated "initial connect" flag in the import instead of relying on imp_remote_handle ?

Patch Set 2:

> to me it looks like we just need a dedicated "initial connect" flag
 > in the import instead of relying on imp_remote_handle ?

Do you mean set ocd_connect_flag unconditional except idle recoonecting?

Patch Set 2:

> well, I guess the original check:
 > if (!lustre_handle_is_used(&imp->imp_remote_handle))
 > initial_connect = 1;
 > doesn't work because disconnect resets imp_remote_handle, right?
 > if so, then we just need to introduce a dedicate bitflag in import
 > to reset the connect data at the very first connect?

So we need a flag in import to determine connecting or reconnecting instead of check remote_handle? Let me check it.

Patch Set 2:

well, I'd hope it will be more clear for readers..

parents.subject: LU-12405 lnet: Oracle OFED extensions default to on

commit.subject: LU-11354 ptlrpc: don't reset ns_connect_flags for idle import
commit.message:
LU-11354 ptlrpc: don't reset ns_connect_flags for idle import

The ns_connect_flags will reset when a new import connecting. But
idle import also be treated as new connecting. This will cause some
user define value lose.

Signed-off-by: Yang Sheng <ys@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: Iee20b9e0b807a1bc1fc96a82d0035dc0a09d1d49
